Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. LLM Architecture Gallery 426 upvotes, 33 comments
  2. A Visual Introduction to Machine Learning (2015) 365 upvotes, 31 comments
  3. Reviewing Large Changes with Jujutsu 20 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. How far can you go with IX Route Servers only? 42 upvotes, 3 comments
  5. The Linux Programming Interface as a university course text 107 upvotes, 14 comments
  6. What every computer scientist should know about floating-point arithmetic (1991) [pdf] 78 upvotes, 12 comments
  7. Electric motor scaling laws and inertia in robot actuators 96 upvotes, 19 comments
  8. Six ingenious ways how Canon DSLRs used to illuminate their autofocus points 45 upvotes, 7 comments
  9. Canada's bill C-22 mandates mass metadata surveillance 752 upvotes, 224 comments
  10. SpiceCrypt: A Python library for decrypting LTspice encrypted model files 44 upvotes, 8 comments
  11. Chrome DevTools MCP (2025) 492 upvotes, 201 comments
  12. Bus travel from Lima to Rio de Janeiro 188 upvotes, 71 comments
  13. Home Assistant waters my plants 53 upvotes, 15 comments
  14. Stop Sloppypasta 386 upvotes, 162 comments
  15. Kona EV Hacking 51 upvotes, 15 comments
  16. The 49MB web page 600 upvotes, 271 comments
  17. //go:fix inline and the source-level inliner 164 upvotes, 68 comments
  18. Separating the Wayland compositor and window manager 307 upvotes, 160 comments
  19. Why Are Viral Capsids Icosahedral? 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  20. Ask HN: What is it like being in a CS major program these days? 61 upvotes, 25 comments
  21. Linux 7.1 to Retire UDP-Lite – Allows for Better Performance with Cleansed Code 16 upvotes, 4 comments
  22. Lies I was told about collaborative editing, Part 2: Why we don't use Yjs 98 upvotes, 51 comments
  23. Glassworm is back: A new wave of invisible Unicode attacks hits repositories 268 upvotes, 165 comments
  24. LLMs can be exhausting 239 upvotes, 159 comments
  25. What makes Intel Optane stand out (2023) 210 upvotes, 148 comments
  26. How I write software with LLMs 245 upvotes, 189 comments
  27. The emergence of print-on-demand Amazon paperback books 171 upvotes, 129 comments
  28. LoKI – Local AI Assistant for Linux and WSL 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  29. Scientists discover a surprising way to quiet the anxious mind (2025) 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. A new Bigfoot documentary helps explain our conspiracy-minded era 68 upvotes, 70 comments

Last generated 7:30pm, 16 March 2026. Source here.
